Entire CLI will link this conversation to your next commit.
  1 other active conversation(s) in this workspace will also be included.
  Use 'entire status' for more information.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# WP-012 — KRN-V3-05: Stair by Sketch
2	
3	**Branch:** `feat/v3-krn-v3-05-stair-by-sketch`
4	**Effort:** L — 2.5 weeks
5	**State:** `now`
6	
7	## Required reading
8	
9	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §6 T1 KRN-V3-05 entry (around line 646)
10	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
11	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/elements.py` — `StairElem`, `StairRun`, `StairLanding`, `Vec2Mm` (lines 585–665)
12	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/commands.py` — `CreateStairCmd` (line 510); existing `shape='sketch'` validator
13	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/engine.py` — `_materialize_stair_runs_and_landings`, `CreateStairCmd` dispatch (lines 371–441, 1952–1979)
14	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/sketch_session.py` — `_emit_stair`, `SUBMODES`, `SketchElementKind` (lines 392–444)
15	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/tests/test_create_stair_spiral_and_sketch.py` — round-trip tests as pattern reference
16	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/plan/SketchCanvas.tsx` — existing sketch overlay component
17	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/plan/sketchApi.ts` — `SketchElementKind`, session API
18	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/core/src/index.ts` — `StairRun`, `StairLanding`, stair element TS types (lines 98–661)
19	
20	## WP-012 verbatim from tracker (§6 T1 KRN-V3-05)
21	
22	**KRN-V3-05 — Stair by sketch**
23	
24	_Source: R-D §3 G8. Severity High. Sketch-mode is what architects actually use for winders / scissor / custom shapes / renovation work._
25	
26	**Status.** `now`.
27	**Scope.** Add a second authoring mode to `Stair`: `kind="by_sketch"`. User draws a 2D boundary polygon + tread lines + riser lines in plan; the kernel extrudes each tread to step height; supports winders (non-parallel tread lines). Component-mode (run + landing + run) stays the v2 default. Sketch-mode is a soft contextual scope per A1 antidote (D8) — the rest of the model stays editable.
28	**Data model.**
29	```ts
30	{
31	  kind: 'stair';
32	  authoringMode: 'by_component' | 'by_sketch';   // existing 'by_component' preserved
33	  // by_sketch fields (only set when authoringMode='by_sketch'):
34	  boundary: Array<{ xMm: number; yMm: number }>;
35	  treadLines: Array<{
36	    fromMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number };
37	    toMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number };
38	    riserHeightMm?: number;    // default = (totalRise / treadCount); editable per-line
39	  }>;
40	  totalRiseMm: number;
41	}
42	```
43	**Engine.** Apply: validate boundary is a simple polygon; tread lines monotonic by stair direction; total tread count + riser sum match `totalRiseMm` ± tolerance. Auto-balance solver: when `riserHeightMm` is null on a tread line, distribute remaining rise equally across nulls; when one tread is dragged, rebalance the others (U2 invariant). Render: extrude each tread cell (between two adjacent tread lines) to its step height; assemble vertical risers between cells.
44	**UI.** Stair tool gains a mode toggle "By component | By sketch". Sketch mode opens a contextual scope: tread-line drawing tool active, comfort advisory chip ("260 × 190" target) live. Drag any tread line → others rebalance live (U2 gold-standard). Comfort advisory follows EU residential proxy (`tread ≥ 260 mm × riser ≤ 190 mm`).
45	**Acceptance.** Author a winder stair in a renovation L-shape: 12 risers, 11 treads with 3 winder treads in the corner; the auto-balance distributes uniform risers; drag the central winder tread, the others rebalance; commit; the stair reads correctly in plan + 3D. Comfort advisory stays green throughout; if it goes red the chip explains why.
46	**Effort.** L — 2.5 weeks.
47	**Cross-theme references.** T2 (U2 auto-balance is the marquee invariant — the gold-standard "rebalance-others-as-you-drag" pattern lives here); T9 (sketch-stair is a tokenisable kind for the agent).
48	
49	## Setup
50	
51	```bash
52	git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only && git checkout -b feat/v3-krn-v3-05-stair-by-sketch
53	```
54	
55	## Implementation tasks
56	
57	### 1. Data model — Python (`elements.py`)
58	
59	Add a `StairTreadLine` Pydantic model and extend `StairElem`:
60	
61	```python
62	class StairTreadLine(BaseModel):
63	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
64	    from_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="fromMm")
65	    to_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="toMm")
66	    riser_height_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="riserHeightMm")
67	```
68	
69	Add to `StairElem` (after the existing `sketch_path_mm` field, before `pinned`):
70	
71	```python
72	authoring_mode: Literal["by_component", "by_sketch"] = Field(
73	    default="by_component", alias="authoringMode"
74	)
75	boundary_mm: list[Vec2Mm] | None = Field(default=None, alias="boundaryMm")
76	tread_lines: list[StairTreadLine] | None = Field(default=None, alias="treadLines")
77	total_rise_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="totalRiseMm")
78	```
79	
80	Extend `_validate_shape_specific_fields` to check: when `authoring_mode == "by_sketch"`, `boundary_mm` must be a closed polygon (≥ 3 points), `tread_lines` must have ≥ 1 entry, and `total_rise_mm` must be positive.
81	
82	### 2. Data model — Python (`commands.py`)
83	
84	Extend `CreateStairCmd` with the same four new fields (`authoringMode`, `boundaryMm`, `treadLines`, `totalRiseMm`). Mirror the validator from `StairElem`. Import `StairTreadLine` from `elements`.
85	
86	### 3. Data model — TypeScript (`packages/core/src/index.ts`)
87	
88	Add `StairTreadLine` export type and extend the stair element union member:
89	
90	```ts
91	export type StairTreadLine = {
92	  fromMm: XY;
93	  toMm: XY;
94	  riserHeightMm?: number | null;
95	};
96	```
97	
98	On the stair element: add `authoringMode?: 'by_component' | 'by_sketch'`, `boundaryMm?: XY[]`, `treadLines?: StairTreadLine[]`, `totalRiseMm?: number`. Mark all four optional so the existing `by_component` wire shape is unchanged.
99	
100	### 4. Engine — auto-balance solver (`engine.py`)
101	
102	Add a pure helper `_balance_tread_risers`:
103	
104	```python
105	def _balance_tread_risers(
106	    tread_lines: list[StairTreadLine],
107	    total_rise_mm: float,
108	) -> list[float]:
109	    """Return a riser height per tread. Lines with riserHeightMm set are
110	    pinned; nulls receive an equal share of the remaining rise."""
111	```
112	
113	Rules:
114	- Sum of all pinned `riserHeightMm` values must not exceed `total_rise_mm` — raise `ValueError` with a clear message if so.
115	- Remaining rise is divided equally across nulls.
116	- Return value has one entry per tread line; order is preserved.
117	
118	### 5. Engine — `_materialize_stair_runs_and_landings` extension (`engine.py`)
119	
120	When `cmd.authoring_mode == "by_sketch"` and `cmd.tread_lines` is set:
121	- Call `_balance_tread_risers` to resolve all riser heights.
122	- For each consecutive pair of tread lines, create a `StairRun` whose `start_mm` is the midpoint of tread line N and `end_mm` is the midpoint of tread line N+1; `riser_count = 1` per cell.
123	- Store `boundary_mm`, `tread_lines` (with `riser_height_mm` filled in from the balanced result), and `total_rise_mm` through to the `StairElem`.
124	
125	### 6. Engine — `CreateStairCmd` dispatch (`engine.py`)
126	
127	In the `case CreateStairCmd():` block, pass `authoring_mode`, `boundary_mm`, `tread_lines`, and `total_rise_mm` through to `StairElem(...)`. The existing `by_component` path is unchanged.
128	
129	### 7. Validation (`engine.py`)
130	
131	Add boundary validation helper `_validate_stair_boundary`:
132	- Rejects fewer than 3 points.
133	- Rejects self-intersecting polygons (use a simple cross-product sweep; no external geometry library).
134	- Rejects zero-area polygons.
135	
136	Call it from `CreateStairCmd` dispatch when `authoring_mode == "by_sketch"`.
137	
138	### 8. Sketch session finish emitter (`sketch_session.py`)
139	
140	Extend `_emit_stair` to support the new mode. When `opts` carries `"authoringMode": "by_sketch"`:
141	- Require `boundaryMm` (≥ 3 points), `treadLines` (≥ 1 entry), and `totalRiseMm` in `opts`.
142	- Emit `createStair` with `authoringMode="by_sketch"` and the new fields.
143	- The existing `shape="sketch"` polyline path continues to work unchanged for `authoringMode="by_component"` callers.
144	
145	### 9. Frontend — `sketchApi.ts`
146	
147	Add `'stair_by_sketch'` to `SketchElementKind`. Add a `StairSketchFinishOpts` type:
148	
149	```ts
150	export type StairSketchFinishOpts = {
151	  topLevelId: string;
152	  baseLevelId?: string;
153	  authoringMode: 'by_sketch';
154	  boundaryMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number }[];
155	  treadLines: { fromMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number }; toMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number }; riserHeightMm?: number }[];
156	  totalRiseMm: number;
157	  name?: string;
158	};
159	```
160	
161	Pass `StairSketchFinishOpts` as `opts` to `finishSketchSession` when `elementKind === 'stair_by_sketch'`.
162	
163	### 10. Frontend — `StairBySketchCanvas.tsx` (new component)
164	
165	Create `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/plan/StairBySketchCanvas.tsx`. Model it on `SketchCanvas.tsx` but replace the generic line-drawing session with two distinct drawing phases:
166	
167	**Phase A — Boundary:** User draws a closed polygon (the stair footprint). Reuse `SketchCanvas` in `elementKind='floor'` mode for polygon capture; do not duplicate the snap / validation logic.
168	
169	**Phase B — Tread lines:** After boundary is committed, overlay switches to tread-line drawing mode. User draws lines across the interior of the boundary. Each line is snapped to the nearest 10 mm grid. A live comfort advisory chip reads current average tread depth and riser height against EU proxy (`tread ≥ 260 mm`, `riser ≤ 190 mm`).
170	
171	The advisory chip must use CSS tokens only — no hex literals. Use `var(--color-success)` for green, `var(--color-destructive)` for red, `var(--text-sm)` for body, `var(--ease-paper)` for state transitions.
172	
173	On Finish: assemble `StairSketchFinishOpts` from the boundary + tread lines + `totalRiseMm` (derived from level elevation difference); call `finishSketchSession` with the assembled opts.
174	
175	### 11. Frontend — stair tool mode toggle
176	
177	In the existing stair placement UI (locate it with `grep -r "createStair\|StairTool\|stairTool" /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/`), add a segmented control "By component | By sketch". When "By sketch" is selected, mount `StairBySketchCanvas` in place of the existing component-mode form. The segmented control is styled with `var(--radius-sm)` border-radius and `var(--text-sm)` label size — no hex.
178	
179	### 12. Tests
180	
181	Write `app/tests/test_stair_by_sketch.py` with ≥ 16 tests:
182	
183	- `test_balance_all_nulls` — all `riserHeightMm` null → equal distribution.
184	- `test_balance_pinned_and_nulls` — 2 pinned + 3 null → nulls share remainder.
185	- `test_balance_pinned_exceeds_total_rise` — must raise `ValueError`.
186	- `test_balance_no_nulls` — all pinned, sum matches total → returns as-is.
187	- `test_create_stair_by_sketch_round_trip` — `CreateStairCmd(authoringMode='by_sketch', ...)` → `try_commit` → `StairElem` has correct fields.
188	- `test_by_sketch_requires_boundary` — missing `boundaryMm` raises.
189	- `test_by_sketch_requires_tread_lines` — missing `treadLines` raises.
190	- `test_by_sketch_requires_positive_total_rise` — `totalRiseMm=0` raises.
191	- `test_boundary_must_have_3_plus_points` — 2-point boundary raises.
192	- `test_boundary_self_intersection_rejected` — figure-8 boundary raises.
193	- `test_winder_stair_12_risers` — 12 tread lines, 3 non-parallel (winder), auto-balance distributes uniformly.
194	- `test_winder_drag_rebalance` — pin one tread's `riserHeightMm`, call `_balance_tread_risers`, verify others rebalance.
195	- `test_sketch_session_stair_by_sketch_emit` — `finish_session` with `authoringMode='by_sketch'` opts emits correct command dict.
196	- `test_sketch_session_stair_by_sketch_requires_boundary` — `finish_session` without `boundaryMm` raises `SketchInvalidError`.
197	- `test_stair_runs_materialised_from_tread_lines` — `_materialize_stair_runs_and_landings` with tread lines creates one run per tread cell.
198	- `test_by_component_path_unchanged` — existing `by_component` round-trip still passes after all changes.
199	
200	Follow the pattern in `test_create_stair_spiral_and_sketch.py` for `try_commit` wiring.
201	
202	## Implementation rules
203	
204	- **API-V3-01 contract:** `authoringMode`, `boundaryMm`, `treadLines`, and `totalRiseMm` are new fields on `createStair`. They must be registered in the tool schema / CLI surface docs if a tool-registry exists; check `app/bim_ai/tool_registry.py` (or equivalent) and add entries if the file exists. Do not silently skip registration.
205	- **No hex literals in chrome.** The `StairBySketchCanvas.tsx` and the mode-toggle control must use only CSS tokens (`var(--…)`). The ESLint `bim-ai/no-hex-in-chrome` rule is active; CI will fail on any literal hex in new `.tsx` files.
206	- **`by_component` backward compat.** Every existing test and wire-format consumer must continue to work. `authoringMode` defaults to `"by_component"` on both the Python `StairElem` and the TS type so pre-existing payloads deserialise without change.
207	- **Pure helpers.** `_balance_tread_risers` and `_validate_stair_boundary` must be pure functions — no side effects, no I/O, no mutation of arguments.
208	- **Pydantic alias discipline.** All new Python fields use `Field(alias="camelCase")` and `model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)` (already set on `StairElem`). The wire format is always camelCase JSON.
209	- **Do not widen `SketchElementKind` in `sketchApi.ts` to a plain `string`** — keep it as a discriminated literal union; add `'stair_by_sketch'` as a new member.
210	
211	## engine.py overlap note — WP-004 merged
212	
213	WP-004 (the renderer/CSG fix that unblocked dormer/sweep/balcony visibility) has already been merged into `main` (commit `71f844c3`). When you branch from `main`, `engine.py` will already contain those changes. Do not re-apply them. If a rebase conflict arises in `_materialize_stair_runs_and_landings` or the `CreateStairCmd` dispatch block, resolve by keeping both the WP-004 fixes and this WP's new `by_sketch` path — they touch different branches of the same `match` statement and should not overlap.
214	
215	## Verify gate
216	
217	```bash
218	# Python
219	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app
220	python -m pytest tests/test_stair_by_sketch.py -v
221	python -m pytest tests/test_create_stair_spiral_and_sketch.py -v   # regression
222	make verify
223	
224	# TypeScript
225	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
226	pnpm tsc --noEmit
227	
228	# ESLint (no-hex-in-chrome)
229	pnpm exec eslint packages/web/src/plan/StairBySketchCanvas.tsx
230	```
231	
232	All checks must pass before committing.
233	
234	## Commit and push
235	
236	```bash
237	git add \
238	  app/bim_ai/elements.py \
239	  app/bim_ai/commands.py \
240	  app/bim_ai/engine.py \
241	  app/bim_ai/sketch_session.py \
242	  app/tests/test_stair_by_sketch.py \
243	  packages/core/src/index.ts \
244	  packages/web/src/plan/sketchApi.ts \
245	  packages/web/src/plan/StairBySketchCanvas.tsx
246	git commit -m "feat(krn): KRN-V3-05 stair by sketch — boundary + tread lines + auto-balance winder support"
247	git push -u origin feat/v3-krn-v3-05-stair-by-sketch
248	```
249	
250	## Final report
251	
252	Paste this back to the orchestrator when done:
253	
254	```
255	WP: WP-012 / KRN-V3-05
256	Branch: feat/v3-krn-v3-05-stair-by-sketch
257	Status: done
258	Tests: <N> passed (test_stair_by_sketch.py) + <N> passed (test_create_stair_spiral_and_sketch.py regression)
259	Files changed: <list key files>
260	Notes: <anything unexpected — merge conflicts, scope adjustments, ESLint exceptions needed>
261	```

**Branch:** `feat/v3-krn-v3-05-stair-by-sketch`
**Effort:** L — 2.5 weeks
**State:** `now`

## Required reading

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §6 T1 KRN-V3-05 entry (around line 646)

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/core/src/index.ts` — `StairRun`, `StairLanding`, stair element TS types (lines 98–661)

## WP-012 verbatim from tracker (§6 T1 KRN-V3-05)

**KRN-V3-05 — Stair by sketch**

_Source: R-D §3 G8. Severity High. Sketch-mode is what architects actually use for winders / scissor / custom shapes / renovation work._

**Status.** `now`.
**Scope.** Add a second authoring mode to `Stair`: `kind="by_sketch"`. User draws a 2D boundary polygon + tread lines + riser lines in plan; the kernel extrudes each tread to step height; supports winders (non-parallel tread lines). Component-mode (run + landing + run) stays the v2 default. Sketch-mode is a soft contextual scope per A1 antidote (D8) — the rest of the model stays editable.
**Data model.**
```ts
{
  kind: 'stair';
  authoringMode: 'by_component' | 'by_sketch';   // existing 'by_component' preserved
  // by_sketch fields (only set when authoringMode='by_sketch'):
  boundary: Array<{ xMm: number; yMm: number }>;
  treadLines: Array<{
    fromMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number };
    toMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number };
    riserHeightMm?: number;    // default = (totalRise / treadCount); editable per-line
  }>;
  totalRiseMm: number;
}
```
**Engine.** Apply: validate boundary is a simple polygon; tread lines monotonic by stair direction; total tread count + riser sum match `totalRiseMm` ± tolerance. Auto-balance solver: when `riserHeightMm` is null on a tread line, distribute remaining rise equally across nulls; when one tread is dragged, rebalance the others (U2 invariant). Render: extrude each tread cell (between two adjacent tread lines) to its step height; assemble vertical risers between cells.
**UI.** Stair tool gains a mode toggle "By component | By sketch". Sketch mode opens a contextual scope: tread-line drawing tool active, comfort advisory chip ("260 × 190" target) live. Drag any tread line → others rebalance live (U2 gold-standard). Comfort advisory follows EU residential proxy (`tread ≥ 260 mm × riser ≤ 190 mm`).
**Acceptance.** Author a winder stair in a renovation L-shape: 12 risers, 11 treads with 3 winder treads in the corner; the auto-balance distributes uniform risers; drag the central winder tread, the others rebalance; commit; the stair reads correctly in plan + 3D. Comfort advisory stays green throughout; if it goes red the chip explains why.
**Effort.** L — 2.5 weeks.
**Cross-theme references.** T2 (U2 auto-balance is the marquee invariant — the gold-standard "rebalance-others-as-you-drag" pattern lives here); T9 (sketch-stair is a tokenisable kind for the agent).

## Setup

```bash
git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only && git checkout -b feat/v3-krn-v3-05-stair-by-sketch
```

## Implementation tasks

### 1. Data model — Python (`elements.py`)

Add a `StairTreadLine` Pydantic model and extend `StairElem`:

```python
class StairTreadLine(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    from_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="fromMm")
    to_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="toMm")
    riser_height_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="riserHeightMm")
```

Add to `StairElem` (after the existing `sketch_path_mm` field, before `pinned`):

```python
authoring_mode: Literal["by_component", "by_sketch"] = Field(
    default="by_component", alias="authoringMode"
)
boundary_mm: list[Vec2Mm] | None = Field(default=None, alias="boundaryMm")
tread_lines: list[StairTreadLine] | None = Field(default=None, alias="treadLines")
total_rise_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="totalRiseMm")
```

Extend `_validate_shape_specific_fields` to check: when `authoring_mode == "by_sketch"`, `boundary_mm` must be a closed polygon (≥ 3 points), `tread_lines` must have ≥ 1 entry, and `total_rise_mm` must be positive.

### 2. Data model — Python (`commands.py`)

Extend `CreateStairCmd` with the same four new fields (`authoringMode`, `boundaryMm`, `treadLines`, `totalRiseMm`). Mirror the validator from `StairElem`. Import `StairTreadLine` from `elements`.

### 3. Data model — TypeScript (`packages/core/src/index.ts`)

Add `StairTreadLine` export type and extend the stair element union member:

```ts
export type StairTreadLine = {
  fromMm: XY;
  toMm: XY;
  riserHeightMm?: number | null;
};
```

On the stair element: add `authoringMode?: 'by_component' | 'by_sketch'`, `boundaryMm?: XY[]`, `treadLines?: StairTreadLine[]`, `totalRiseMm?: number`. Mark all four optional so the existing `by_component` wire shape is unchanged.

### 4. Engine — auto-balance solver (`engine.py`)

Add a pure helper `_balance_tread_risers`:

```python
def _balance_tread_risers(
    tread_lines: list[StairTreadLine],
    total_rise_mm: float,
) -> list[float]:
    """Return a riser height per tread. Lines with riserHeightMm set are
    pinned; nulls receive an equal share of the remaining rise."""
```

Rules:
- Sum of all pinned `riserHeightMm` values must not exceed `total_rise_mm` — raise `ValueError` with a clear message if so.
- Remaining rise is divided equally across nulls.
- Return value has one entry per tread line; order is preserved.

### 5. Engine — `_materialize_stair_runs_and_landings` extension (`engine.py`)

When `cmd.authoring_mode == "by_sketch"` and `cmd.tread_lines` is set:
- Call `_balance_tread_risers` to resolve all riser heights.
- For each consecutive pair of tread lines, create a `StairRun` whose `start_mm` is the midpoint of tread line N and `end_mm` is the midpoint of tread line N+1; `riser_count = 1` per cell.
- Store `boundary_mm`, `tread_lines` (with `riser_height_mm` filled in from the balanced result), and `total_rise_mm` through to the `StairElem`.

### 6. Engine — `CreateStairCmd` dispatch (`engine.py`)

In the `case CreateStairCmd():` block, pass `authoring_mode`, `boundary_mm`, `tread_lines`, and `total_rise_mm` through to `StairElem(...)`. The existing `by_component` path is unchanged.

### 7. Validation (`engine.py`)

Add boundary validation helper `_validate_stair_boundary`:
- Rejects fewer than 3 points.
- Rejects self-intersecting polygons (use a simple cross-product sweep; no external geometry library).
- Rejects zero-area polygons.

Call it from `CreateStairCmd` dispatch when `authoring_mode == "by_sketch"`.

### 8. Sketch session finish emitter (`sketch_session.py`)

Extend `_emit_stair` to support the new mode. When `opts` carries `"authoringMode": "by_sketch"`:
- Require `boundaryMm` (≥ 3 points), `treadLines` (≥ 1 entry), and `totalRiseMm` in `opts`.
- Emit `createStair` with `authoringMode="by_sketch"` and the new fields.
- The existing `shape="sketch"` polyline path continues to work unchanged for `authoringMode="by_component"` callers.

### 9. Frontend — `sketchApi.ts`

Add `'stair_by_sketch'` to `SketchElementKind`. Add a `StairSketchFinishOpts` type:

```ts
export type StairSketchFinishOpts = {
  topLevelId: string;
  baseLevelId?: string;
  authoringMode: 'by_sketch';
  boundaryMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number }[];
  treadLines: { fromMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number }; toMm: { xMm: number; yMm: number }; riserHeightMm?: number }[];
  totalRiseMm: number;
  name?: string;
};
```

Pass `StairSketchFinishOpts` as `opts` to `finishSketchSession` when `elementKind === 'stair_by_sketch'`.

### 10. Frontend — `StairBySketchCanvas.tsx` (new component)

Create `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/plan/StairBySketchCanvas.tsx`. Model it on `SketchCanvas.tsx` but replace the generic line-drawing session with two distinct drawing phases:

**Phase A — Boundary:** User draws a closed polygon (the stair footprint). Reuse `SketchCanvas` in `elementKind='floor'` mode for polygon capture; do not duplicate the snap / validation logic.

**Phase B — Tread lines:** After boundary is committed, overlay switches to tread-line drawing mode. User draws lines across the interior of the boundary. Each line is snapped to the nearest 10 mm grid. A live comfort advisory chip reads current average tread depth and riser height against EU proxy (`tread ≥ 260 mm`, `riser ≤ 190 mm`).

The advisory chip must use CSS tokens only — no hex literals. Use `var(--color-success)` for green, `var(--color-destructive)` for red, `var(--text-sm)` for body, `var(--ease-paper)` for state transitions.

On Finish: assemble `StairSketchFinishOpts` from the boundary + tread lines + `totalRiseMm` (derived from level elevation difference); call `finishSketchSession` with the assembled opts.

### 11. Frontend — stair tool mode toggle

In the existing stair placement UI (locate it with `grep -r "createStair\|StairTool\|stairTool" /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/`), add a segmented control "By component | By sketch". When "By sketch" is selected, mount `StairBySketchCanvas` in place of the existing component-mode form. The segmented control is styled with `var(--radius-sm)` border-radius and `var(--text-sm)` label size — no hex.

### 12. Tests

Write `app/tests/test_stair_by_sketch.py` with ≥ 16 tests:

- `test_balance_all_nulls` — all `riserHeightMm` null → equal distribution.
- `test_balance_pinned_and_nulls` — 2 pinned + 3 null → nulls share remainder.
- `test_balance_pinned_exceeds_total_rise` — must raise `ValueError`.
- `test_balance_no_nulls` — all pinned, sum matches total → returns as-is.
- `test_create_stair_by_sketch_round_trip` — `CreateStairCmd(authoringMode='by_sketch', ...)` → `try_commit` → `StairElem` has correct fields.
- `test_by_sketch_requires_boundary` — missing `boundaryMm` raises.
- `test_by_sketch_requires_tread_lines` — missing `treadLines` raises.
- `test_by_sketch_requires_positive_total_rise` — `totalRiseMm=0` raises.
- `test_boundary_must_have_3_plus_points` — 2-point boundary raises.
- `test_boundary_self_intersection_rejected` — figure-8 boundary raises.
- `test_winder_stair_12_risers` — 12 tread lines, 3 non-parallel (winder), auto-balance distributes uniformly.
- `test_winder_drag_rebalance` — pin one tread's `riserHeightMm`, call `_balance_tread_risers`, verify others rebalance.
- `test_sketch_session_stair_by_sketch_emit` — `finish_session` with `authoringMode='by_sketch'` opts emits correct command dict.
- `test_sketch_session_stair_by_sketch_requires_boundary` — `finish_session` without `boundaryMm` raises `SketchInvalidError`.
- `test_stair_runs_materialised_from_tread_lines` — `_materialize_stair_runs_and_landings` with tread lines creates one run per tread cell.
- `test_by_component_path_unchanged` — existing `by_component` round-trip still passes after all changes.

Follow the pattern in `test_create_stair_spiral_and_sketch.py` for `try_commit` wiring.

## Implementation rules

- **API-V3-01 contract:** `authoringMode`, `boundaryMm`, `treadLines`, and `totalRiseMm` are new fields on `createStair`. They must be registered in the tool schema / CLI surface docs if a tool-registry exists; check `app/bim_ai/tool_registry.py` (or equivalent) and add entries if the file exists. Do not silently skip registration.
- **No hex literals in chrome.** The `StairBySketchCanvas.tsx` and the mode-toggle control must use only CSS tokens (`var(--…)`). The ESLint `bim-ai/no-hex-in-chrome` rule is active; CI will fail on any literal hex in new `.tsx` files.
- **`by_component` backward compat.** Every existing test and wire-format consumer must continue to work. `authoringMode` defaults to `"by_component"` on both the Python `StairElem` and the TS type so pre-existing payloads deserialise without change.
- **Pure helpers.** `_balance_tread_risers` and `_validate_stair_boundary` must be pure functions — no side effects, no I/O, no mutation of arguments.
- **Pydantic alias discipline.** All new Python fields use `Field(alias="camelCase")` and `model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)` (already set on `StairElem`). The wire format is always camelCase JSON.
- **Do not widen `SketchElementKind` in `sketchApi.ts` to a plain `string`** — keep it as a discriminated literal union; add `'stair_by_sketch'` as a new member.

## engine.py overlap note — WP-004 merged

WP-004 (the renderer/CSG fix that unblocked dormer/sweep/balcony visibility) has already been merged into `main` (commit `71f844c3`). When you branch from `main`, `engine.py` will already contain those changes. Do not re-apply them. If a rebase conflict arises in `_materialize_stair_runs_and_landings` or the `CreateStairCmd` dispatch block, resolve by keeping both the WP-004 fixes and this WP's new `by_sketch` path — they touch different branches of the same `match` statement and should not overlap.

```bash
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app
python -m pytest tests/test_stair_by_sketch.py -v
python -m pytest tests/test_create_stair_spiral_and_sketch.py -v   # regression
make verify

# TypeScript
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
pnpm tsc --noEmit

# ESLint (no-hex-in-chrome)
pnpm exec eslint packages/web/src/plan/StairBySketchCanvas.tsx
```

All checks must pass before committing.

## Commit and push

```bash
git add \
  app/bim_ai/elements.py \
  app/bim_ai/commands.py \
  app/bim_ai/engine.py \
  app/bim_ai/sketch_session.py \
  app/tests/test_stair_by_sketch.py \
  packages/core/src/index.ts \
  packages/web/src/plan/sketchApi.ts \

git commit -m "feat(krn): KRN-V3-05 stair by sketch — boundary + tread lines + auto-balance winder support"
git push -u origin feat/v3-krn-v3-05-stair-by-sketch
```

Paste this back to the orchestrator when done:

class StairRun(BaseModel):
    """KRN-07: one flight in a multi-run stair.

    Straight runs use start_mm/end_mm. Curved runs (spiral, sketch) populate
    polyline_mm with ≥2 plan-coordinate points; renderers read polyline_mm when
    present and fall back to start/end otherwise.

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)

    start_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="startMm")
    end_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="endMm")
    width_mm: float = Field(alias="widthMm", default=1000)
    riser_count: int = Field(alias="riserCount", default=8)
    polyline_mm: list[Vec2Mm] | None = Field(default=None, alias="polylineMm")

                missing.append("innerRadiusMm")

            if self.total_rotation_deg is None:

def _validate_plan_tag_style_ref(
    els: dict[str, Element],
    ref: str | None,
    lane: Literal["opening", "room"],
) -> None:
    if not ref:
        return
    el = els.get(ref)
    if not isinstance(el, PlanTagStyleElem):
        raise ValueError("plan tag style ref must reference plan_tag_style")
    if el.tag_target != lane:
        raise ValueError(
            f"plan tag style targets '{el.tag_target}' but this slot expects '{lane}' elements"
        )


def _stripped_optional_str(val: str | None) -> str | None:
    if val is None:
        return None
    t = val.strip()
    return t or None


def _materialize_stair_runs_and_landings(
    cmd: CreateStairCmd,
) -> tuple[list[StairRun], list[StairLanding]]:
    """KRN-07: derive runs/landings from a CreateStairCmd.

    - Explicit runs[] passed by the caller win.
    - For shape='straight' with no runs, derive a single run from runStartMm/runEndMm.
    - For shape='l_shape'/'u_shape' with caller-supplied runs but no landings,
      auto-derive a rectangular landing per gap from adjacent run endpoints + width.
    """

    runs = list(cmd.runs)
    landings = list(cmd.landings)

    if not runs and cmd.shape == "straight":
        runs = [
            StairRun(
                id=f"{cmd.id or 'stair'}-run-1",
                start_mm=cmd.run_start_mm,
                end_mm=cmd.run_end_mm,
                width_mm=cmd.width_mm,
                riser_count=8,
            )
        ]

    if cmd.shape in ("l_shape", "u_shape") and runs and not landings:
        for i in range(len(runs) - 1):
            a = runs[i]
            b = runs[i + 1]
            landings.append(_auto_landing_for_gap(f"{cmd.id or 'stair'}-landing-{i + 1}", a, b))

    if cmd.shape == "spiral" and not runs:
        assert cmd.center_mm is not None
        assert cmd.inner_radius_mm is not None
        assert cmd.outer_radius_mm is not None
        assert cmd.total_rotation_deg is not None
        assert cmd.riser_count is not None and cmd.riser_count >= 1
        polyline = _spiral_polyline(
            cmd.center_mm,
            cmd.inner_radius_mm,
            cmd.outer_radius_mm,
            cmd.total_rotation_deg,
            cmd.riser_count,
        )
        runs = [
            StairRun(
                id=f"{cmd.id or 'stair'}-run-1",
                start_mm=polyline[0],
                end_mm=polyline[-1],
                width_mm=cmd.outer_radius_mm - cmd.inner_radius_mm,
                riser_count=cmd.riser_count,
                polyline_mm=polyline,
            )
        ]

    if cmd.shape == "sketch" and not runs:
        assert cmd.sketch_path_mm is not None and len(cmd.sketch_path_mm) >= 2
        polyline = list(cmd.sketch_path_mm)
        riser_count = cmd.riser_count if cmd.riser_count else max(len(polyline) - 1, 1)
        runs = [
            StairRun(
                id=f"{cmd.id or 'stair'}-run-1",
                start_mm=polyline[0],
                end_mm=polyline[-1],
                width_mm=cmd.width_mm,
                riser_count=riser_count,
                polyline_mm=polyline,
            )
        ]

    return runs, landings


def _spiral_polyline(
    center: Vec2Mm,
    inner_r: float,
    outer_r: float,
    total_rot_deg: float,
    riser_count: int,
) -> list[Vec2Mm]:
    """Sample the spiral midline (between inner and outer radius) at riser_count+1 points."""

    import math

    midline_r = (inner_r + outer_r) * 0.5
    total_rot_rad = math.radians(total_rot_deg)
    out: list[Vec2Mm] = []
    for i in range(riser_count + 1):
        t = i / riser_count
        theta = total_rot_rad * t
        out.append(
            Vec2Mm(
                xMm=center.x_mm + midline_r * math.cos(theta),
                yMm=center.y_mm + midline_r * math.sin(theta),
            )
        )
    return out

def _emit_stair(session: SketchSession, opts: dict[str, Any]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    """KRN-07 closeout — emit a CreateStair with shape='sketch' from the session
    line set. The polyline runs through every line's `from_mm` plus the last
    line's `to_mm` (preserving authoring order).
    """

    from bim_ai.sketch_validation import SketchInvalidError

    base_level_id = opts.get("baseLevelId") or session.level_id
    top_level_id = opts.get("topLevelId")
    if not top_level_id:
        raise SketchInvalidError(
            "missing_top_level",
            "stair Finish requires `topLevelId` in options.",
        )

    if not session.lines:
        raise SketchInvalidError(
            "empty_sketch",
            "stair Finish requires at least one sketch line.",
        )

    path: list[dict[str, float]] = []
    for ln in session.lines:
        path.append({"xMm": ln.from_mm.x_mm, "yMm": ln.from_mm.y_mm})
    last = session.lines[-1]
    path.append({"xMm": last.to_mm.x_mm, "yMm": last.to_mm.y_mm})

    cmd: dict[str, Any] = {
        "type": "createStair",
        "name": opts.get("name", "Stair"),
        "baseLevelId": base_level_id,
        "topLevelId": top_level_id,
        "runStartMm": path[0],
        "runEndMm": path[-1],
        "shape": "sketch",
        "sketchPathMm": path,
    }
    for k in ("widthMm", "riserMm", "treadMm", "riserCount"):
        if k in opts and opts[k] is not None:
            cmd[k] = opts[k]
    return [cmd]


SUBMODES: dict[str, SubmodeSpec] = {
    "floor": SubmodeSpec(_validate_polygon_session, _emit_floor),
    "roof": SubmodeSpec(_validate_polygon_session, _emit_roof),
    "room_separation": SubmodeSpec(_validate_line_set_session, _emit_room_separation),
    "ceiling": SubmodeSpec(_validate_polygon_session, _emit_ceiling),
    "in_place_mass": SubmodeSpec(_validate_polygon_session, _emit_in_place_mass),
    "void_cut": SubmodeSpec(_validate_polygon_session, _emit_void_cut),
    "detail_region": SubmodeSpec(_validate_line_set_session, _emit_detail_region),
    "stair": SubmodeSpec(_validate_line_set_session, _emit_stair),
}

file.content:
"""KRN-07 closeout: spiral + sketch shape variants for CreateStair."""

from __future__ import annotations

import math

import pytest

from bim_ai.commands import CreateLevelCmd, CreateStairCmd
from bim_ai.document import Document
from bim_ai.elements import StairElem
from bim_ai.engine import apply_inplace, try_commit
from bim_ai.sketch_session import SketchLine, SketchSession, finish_session
from bim_ai.sketch_validation import SketchInvalidError


def _doc_with_levels() -> Document:
    doc = Document(revision=1, elements={})
    apply_inplace(doc, CreateLevelCmd(id="lvl-0", name="L0", elevationMm=0))
    apply_inplace(doc, CreateLevelCmd(id="lvl-1", name="L1", elevationMm=2800))
    return doc


def test_spiral_stair_derives_n_treads() -> None:
    """Spiral stair: 12 risers around a 270° rotation should produce a single curved-run
    record with a 13-vertex polyline (riserCount + 1 sample points)."""

    doc = _doc_with_levels()
    apply_inplace(
        doc,
        CreateStairCmd(
            id="s-spiral",
            base_level_id="lvl-0",
            top_level_id="lvl-1",
            run_start_mm={"xMm": 0, "yMm": 0},
            run_end_mm={"xMm": 0, "yMm": 0},
            shape="spiral",
            centerMm={"xMm": 0, "yMm": 0},
            innerRadiusMm=200,
            outerRadiusMm=1200,
            totalRotationDeg=270,
            riserCount=12,
        ),
    )
    s = doc.elements["s-spiral"]
    assert isinstance(s, StairElem)
    assert s.shape == "spiral"
    assert len(s.runs) == 1
    assert s.runs[0].polyline_mm is not None
    assert len(s.runs[0].polyline_mm) == 13
    midR = (200 + 1200) / 2
    first = s.runs[0].polyline_mm[0]
    assert first.x_mm == pytest.approx(midR, abs=1e-6)
    assert first.y_mm == pytest.approx(0, abs=1e-6)
    last = s.runs[0].polyline_mm[-1]
    expected_x = midR * math.cos(math.radians(270))
    expected_y = midR * math.sin(math.radians(270))
    assert last.x_mm == pytest.approx(expected_x, abs=1e-6)
    assert last.y_mm == pytest.approx(expected_y, abs=1e-6)


def test_spiral_validates_required_fields() -> None:
    """Missing centerMm on a spiral stair must raise."""

    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="centerMm"):
        CreateStairCmd(
            id="s-bad-spiral",
            base_level_id="lvl-0",
            top_level_id="lvl-1",
            run_start_mm={"xMm": 0, "yMm": 0},
            run_end_mm={"xMm": 0, "yMm": 0},
            shape="spiral",
            innerRadiusMm=200,
            outerRadiusMm=1200,
            totalRotationDeg=270,
            riserCount=12,
        )


def test_spiral_outer_must_exceed_inner() -> None:

file.content:
"""SKT-01 — `SketchSession` transient state machine.

Sessions are server-side scratchpads for sketch-mode authoring: lines, vertices,
and validation state are tracked in-memory until `Finish` translates the closed
loop into a single persisted command (e.g. `CreateFloor`). Sessions never enter
the `Document` snapshot — they have no element id, no undo entry, no IFC trace.

Element kinds supported: `floor`, `roof`, `room_separation`, `ceiling`,
`in_place_mass`, `void_cut`, `detail_region`. The first three came in the
wave2-3/wave3-4 slices; the latter four landed in wave-04 (this file).

Each sub-mode is registered in :data:`SUBMODES` with a validator and a
Finish-emitter; :func:`finish_session` is the single dispatch entry point that
the HTTP route calls.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import uuid
from collections.abc import Callable
from dataclasses import dataclass
from threading import RLock
from typing import Any, Literal

from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field

from bim_ai.elements import Vec2Mm

SketchElementKind = Literal[
    "floor",
    "roof",
    "room_separation",
    "ceiling",
    "in_place_mass",
    "void_cut",
    "detail_region",
    "stair",
]
SketchSessionStatus = Literal["open", "finished", "cancelled"]
# SKT-02: sub-tool config — does Pick Walls insert wall centerlines or
# offset-by-half-thickness lines along the wall's interior face?
PickWallsOffsetMode = Literal["centerline", "interior_face"]


class SketchLine(BaseModel):
    """A single straight segment in a sketch session, in level-local mm."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    from_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="fromMm")
    to_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="toMm")


class SketchValidationIssue(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    code: str
    message: str
    line_index: int | None = Field(default=None, alias="lineIndex")
    line_indices: list[int] | None = Field(default=None, alias="lineIndices")

content.content:
448	def finish_session(
449	    session: SketchSession,
450	    options: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
451	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
452	    """Validate the session for its sub-mode, then return the Finish commands.
453	
454	    Raises :class:`SketchInvalidError` on validation failure (open loop /
455	    empty line set / missing required option). Caller is responsible for
456	    feeding the returned commands through `try_commit` and persisting the
457	    document.
458	    """
459	
460	    spec = SUBMODES.get(session.element_kind)
461	    if spec is None:
462	        raise ValueError(f"unsupported element_kind: {session.element_kind!r}")
463	
464	    spec.validator(session)
465	
466	    merged: dict[str, Any] = {}
467	    merged.update(session.options or {})
468	    if options:
469	        merged.update(options)
470	
471	    return spec.emitter(session, merged)
472

1	"""SKT-01 — sketch session HTTP API (floor-only slice).
2	
3	Sketch sessions are transient scratchpads, not persisted commands. They live in
4	:class:`SketchSessionRegistry` (in-process) and only emit a single persisted
5	`CreateFloor` on `Finish`. That commit goes through the regular `try_commit`
6	path so undo/redo, constraint evaluation, and WebSocket broadcasts all work as
7	they do for any other authoring command.
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	from datetime import UTC, datetime
13	from typing import Any
14	from uuid import UUID
15	
16	from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException
17	from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field
18	from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
19	
20	from bim_ai.db import get_session
21	from bim_ai.document import Document
22	from bim_ai.elements import LevelElem, Vec2Mm, WallElem
23	from bim_ai.engine import (
24	    clone_document,
25	    compute_delta_wire,
26	    diff_undo_cmds,
27	    try_commit,
28	)
29	from bim_ai.hub import Hub
30	from bim_ai.routes_deps import (
31	    delete_redos,
32	    document_to_wire,
33	    get_hub,
34	    load_model_row,
35	    violations_wire,
36	)
37	from bim_ai.sketch_pick_walls import (
38	    rebuild_picked_walls_lines,
39	)
40	from bim_ai.sketch_session import (
41	    SUBMODES,
42	    PickedWall,
43	    PickWallsOffsetMode,
44	    SketchLine,
45	    SketchSession,
46	    SketchValidationState,
47	    finish_session,
48	    get_sketch_registry,
49	)
50	from bim_ai.sketch_validation import (
51	    SketchInvalidError,
52	    validate_sketch_session,
53	)
54	from bim_ai.tables import UndoStackRecord
55	
56	sketch_router = APIRouter()
57	
58	
59	def _session_payload(session: SketchSession, validation: SketchValidationState) -> dict[str, Any]:
60	    return {
61	        "session": session.model_dump(by_alias=True),
62	        "validation": validation.model_dump(by_alias=True),
63	    }
64	
65	
66	# --- Request envelopes ----------------------------------------------------------------
67	
68	
69	class OpenSketchSessionRequest(BaseModel):
70	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
71	    model_id: UUID = Field(alias="modelId")
72	    element_kind: str = Field(default="floor", alias="elementKind")
73	    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
74	    pick_walls_offset_mode: PickWallsOffsetMode = Field(
75	        default="interior_face", alias="pickWallsOffsetMode"
76	    )
77	    options: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
78	
79	
80	class AddSketchLineRequest(BaseModel):
81	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
82	    from_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="fromMm")
83	    to_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="toMm")
84	
85	
86	class RemoveSketchLineRequest(BaseModel):
87	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
88	    line_index: int = Field(alias="lineIndex")
89	
90	
91	class MoveSketchVertexRequest(BaseModel):
92	    """Move every endpoint coincident with `fromMm` to `toMm`.
93	
94	    Coincidence is decided by the sketch-validation vertex tolerance (sub-mm)
95	    so two lines that share a vertex stay glued after the move.
96	    """
97	
98	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
99	    from_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="fromMm")
100	    to_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="toMm")
101	
102	
103	class PickWallRequest(BaseModel):
104	    """SKT-02 — toggle a wall in/out of the sketch session.
105	
106	    If the wall id is already among the session's `picked_walls`, it's removed
107	    (toggle off). Otherwise the wall's centerline (or interior-face offset)
108	    is added as a sketch line. After every toggle the registry re-derives all
109	    picked-wall lines so a flipped offset mode or a freshly added neighbour
110	    triggers a corner re-trim.
111	    """
112	
113	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
114	    wall_id: str = Field(alias="wallId")
115	
116	
117	class SetPickWallsOffsetModeRequest(BaseModel):
118	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
119	    mode: PickWallsOffsetMode
120	
121	
122	class FinishSketchSessionRequest(BaseModel):
123	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
124	    name: str = Field(default="Floor")
125	    floor_type_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="floorTypeId")
126	    thickness_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="thicknessMm")
127	    user_id: str | None = Field(default="local-dev", alias="userId")
128	    client_op_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="clientOpId")
129	    # Generic sub-mode options bag — overlaid on top of `session.options` and
130	    # forwarded to the per-kind Finish-emitter. Lets one Finish endpoint
131	    # serve every supported `element_kind` without per-kind request models.
132	    options: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
133	
134	
135	# --- Endpoints ------------------------------------------------------------------------
136	
137	
138	# Wave-04 SKT-01 closeout: every `SketchElementKind` is now wired through a
139	# SubmodeSpec (validator + Finish-emitter). The route accepts whatever the
140	# session-side registry advertises so adding a new sub-mode is a one-place edit.
141	_SUPPORTED_ELEMENT_KINDS = set(SUBMODES.keys())
142	
143	
144	@sketch_router.post("/sketch-sessions")
145	async def open_sketch_session(
146	    body: OpenSketchSessionRequest,
147	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
148	) -> dict[str, Any]:
149	    if body.element_kind not in _SUPPORTED_ELEMENT_KINDS:
150	        raise HTTPException(
151	            status_code=400,
152	            detail=(
153	                f"unsupported_element_kind: '{body.element_kind}' — supported: "
154	                f"{sorted(_SUPPORTED_ELEMENT_KINDS)}"
155	            ),
156	        )
157	
158	    row = await load_model_row(session, body.model_id)
159	    if row is None:
160	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
161	
162	    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
163	    level = doc.elements.get(body.level_id)
164	    if not isinstance(level, LevelElem):
165	        raise HTTPException(
166	            status_code=400,
167	            detail=f"level_id '{body.level_id}' must reference an existing Level",
168	        )
169	
170	    sk = get_sketch_registry().open(
171	        model_id=str(body.model_id),
172	        element_kind=body.element_kind,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
173	        level_id=body.level_id,
174	        pick_walls_offset_mode=body.pick_walls_offset_mode,
175	        options=body.options,
176	    )
177	    return _session_payload(sk, validate_sketch_session(sk))
178	
179	
180	@sketch_router.get("/sketch-sessions/{session_id}")
181	async def get_sketch_session(session_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
182	    sk = get_sketch_registry().get(session_id)
183	    if sk is None:
184	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="sketch session not found")
185	    return _session_payload(sk, validate_sketch_session(sk))
186	
187	
188	@sketch_router.post("/sketch-sessions/{session_id}/lines")
189	async def add_sketch_line(session_id: str, body: AddSketchLineRequest) -> dict[str, Any]:
190	    reg = get_sketch_registry()
191	    try:
192	        sk = reg.require_open(session_id)
193	    except KeyError as exc:
194	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=str(exc)) from exc
195	    except ValueError as exc:
196	        raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail=str(exc)) from exc
197	    sk = sk.model_copy(
198	        update={"lines": [*sk.lines, SketchLine(from_mm=body.from_mm, to_mm=body.to_mm)]}
199	    )
200	    reg.replace(sk)
201	    return _session_payload(sk, validate_sketch_session(sk))
202	
203	
204	@sketch_router.post("/sketch-sessions/{session_id}/remove-line")
205	async def remove_sketch_line(session_id: str, body: RemoveSketchLineRequest) -> dict[str, Any]:
206	    reg = get_sketch_registry()
207	    try:
208	        sk = reg.require_open(session_id)
209	    except KeyError as exc:
210	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=str(exc)) from exc
211	    except ValueError as exc:
212	        raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail=str(exc)) from exc
213	    if body.line_index < 0 or body.line_index >= len(sk.lines):
214	        raise HTTPException(
215	            status_code=400,
216	            detail=f"line_index {body.line_index} out of range (0..{len(sk.lines) - 1})",
217	        )
218	    new_lines = list(sk.lines)
219	    new_lines.pop(body.line_index)
220	    sk = sk.model_copy(update={"lines": new_lines})
221	    reg.replace(sk)
222	    return _session_payload(sk, validate_sketch_session(sk))
223	
224	
225	@sketch_router.post("/sketch-sessions/{session_id}/move-vertex")
226	async def move_sketch_vertex(session_id: str, body: MoveSketchVertexRequest) -> dict[str, Any]:
227	    reg = get_sketch_registry()
228	    try:
229	        sk = reg.require_open(session_id)
230	    except KeyError as exc:
231	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=str(exc)) from exc
232	    except ValueError as exc:
233	        raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail=str(exc)) from exc
234	
235	    eps_mm = 0.5
236	
237	    def _coincident(a: Vec2Mm) -> bool:
238	        return (
239	            abs(a.x_mm - body.from_mm.x_mm) <= eps_mm and abs(a.y_mm - body.from_mm.y_mm) <= eps_mm
240	        )
241	
242	    new_lines: list[SketchLine] = []
243	    for line in sk.lines:
244	        new_from = body.to_mm if _coincident(line.from_mm) else line.from_mm
245	        new_to = body.to_mm if _coincident(line.to_mm) else line.to_mm
246	        new_lines.append(SketchLine(from_mm=new_from, to_mm=new_to))
247	    sk = sk.model_copy(update={"lines": new_lines})
248	    reg.replace(sk)
249	    return _session_payload(sk, validate_sketch_session(sk))
250	
251	
252	@sketch_router.post("/sketch-sessions/{session_id}/pick-wall")
253	async def pick_wall(
254	    session_id: str,
255	    body: PickWallRequest,
256	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
257	) -> dict[str, Any]:
258	    reg = get_sketch_registry()
259	    try:
260	        sk = reg.require_open(session_id)
261	    except KeyError as exc:
262	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=str(exc)) from exc
263	    except ValueError as exc:
264	        raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail=str(exc)) from exc
265	
266	    row = await load_model_row(session, UUID(sk.model_id))
267	    if row is None:
268	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
269	    doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
270	    wall = doc.elements.get(body.wall_id)
271	    if not isinstance(wall, WallElem):
272	        raise HTTPException(
273	            status_code=400,
274	            detail=f"wall_id '{body.wall_id}' must reference an existing wall",
275	        )
276	
277	    walls_by_id: dict[str, WallElem] = {
278	        eid: el for eid, el in doc.elements.items() if isinstance(el, WallElem)
279	    }
280	
281	    already_picked = next((p for p in sk.picked_walls if p.wall_id == body.wall_id), None)
282	    if already_picked is not None:
283	        new_picked = [p for p in sk.picked_walls if p.wall_id != body.wall_id]
284	    else:
285	        new_picked = [
286	            *sk.picked_walls,
287	            PickedWall(wall_id=body.wall_id, line_index=-1),
288	        ]
289	    sk = sk.model_copy(update={"picked_walls": new_picked})
290	    new_lines, repinned = rebuild_picked_walls_lines(sk, walls_by_id)
291	    sk = sk.model_copy(update={"lines": new_lines, "picked_walls": repinned})
292	    reg.replace(sk)
293	    return _session_payload(sk, validate_sketch_session(sk))
294	
295	
296	@sketch_router.post("/sketch-sessions/{session_id}/pick-walls-offset-mode")
297	async def set_pick_walls_offset_mode(
298	    session_id: str,
299	    body: SetPickWallsOffsetModeRequest,
300	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
301	) -> dict[str, Any]:
302	    reg = get_sketch_registry()
303	    try:
304	        sk = reg.require_open(session_id)
305	    except KeyError as exc:
306	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=str(exc)) from exc
307	    except ValueError as exc:
308	        raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail=str(exc)) from exc
309	
310	    sk = sk.model_copy(update={"pick_walls_offset_mode": body.mode})
311	    if sk.picked_walls:
312	        row = await load_model_row(session, UUID(sk.model_id))
313	        if row is None:
314	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
315	        doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
316	        walls_by_id: dict[str, WallElem] = {
317	            eid: el for eid, el in doc.elements.items() if isinstance(el, WallElem)
318	        }
319	        new_lines, repinned = rebuild_picked_walls_lines(sk, walls_by_id)
320	        sk = sk.model_copy(update={"lines": new_lines, "picked_walls": repinned})
321	    reg.replace(sk)
322	    return _session_payload(sk, validate_sketch_session(sk))
323	
324	
325	@sketch_router.post("/sketch-sessions/{session_id}/cancel")
326	async def cancel_sketch_session(session_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
327	    reg = get_sketch_registry()
328	    sk = reg.get(session_id)
329	    if sk is None:
330	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="sketch session not found")
331	    sk = sk.model_copy(update={"status": "cancelled"})
332	    reg.replace(sk)
333	    reg.discard(session_id)
334	    return {"ok": True, "sessionId": session_id, "status": "cancelled"}
335	
336	
337	def _build_finish_commands(
338	    sk: SketchSession,
339	    body: FinishSketchSessionRequest,
340	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
341	    """Thin adapter around :func:`finish_session`.
342	
343	    Pulls per-kind option overrides off the request body (back-compat for the
344	    explicit `name` / `floorTypeId` / `thicknessMm` fields), merges them onto
345	    the generic `options` dict, and lets the sub-mode dispatch produce the
346	    commands. Validation errors raised by the validator or emitter are
347	    re-shaped into 409 HTTPException for the route caller.
348	    """
349	
350	    overrides: dict[str, Any] = dict(body.options or {})
351	    if body.name and "name" not in overrides:
352	        overrides["name"] = body.name
353	    if body.floor_type_id is not None and "floorTypeId" not in overrides:
354	        overrides["floorTypeId"] = body.floor_type_id
355	    if body.thickness_mm is not None and "thicknessMm" not in overrides:
356	        overrides["thicknessMm"] = body.thickness_mm
357	
358	    try:
359	        return finish_session(sk, overrides)
360	    except SketchInvalidError as exc:
361	        raise HTTPException(
362	            status_code=409,
363	            detail={
364	                "reason": "sketch_invalid",
365	                "validation": {
366	                    "valid": False,
367	                    "issues": [{"code": exc.code, "message": exc.message}],
368	                },
369	            },
370	        ) from exc
371	
372	
373	_KIND_TO_NEW_ID_FIELD: dict[str, tuple[str, str]] = {
374	    "floor": ("floor", "floorId"),
375	    "roof": ("roof", "roofId"),
376	    "room_separation": ("room_separation", "roomSeparationId"),
377	    "ceiling": ("ceiling", "ceilingId"),
378	    "in_place_mass": ("mass", "massId"),
379	    "void_cut": ("void_cut", "voidCutId"),
380	    "detail_region": ("detail_region", "detailRegionId"),
381	}
382	
383	
384	@sketch_router.post("/sketch-sessions/{session_id}/finish")
385	async def finish_sketch_session(
386	    session_id: str,
387	    body: FinishSketchSessionRequest,
388	    session: AsyncSession = Depends(get_session),
389	    hub: Hub = Depends(get_hub),
390	) -> dict[str, Any]:
391	    reg = get_sketch_registry()
392	    try:
393	        sk = reg.require_open(session_id)
394	    except KeyError as exc:
395	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=str(exc)) from exc
396	    except ValueError as exc:
397	        raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail=str(exc)) from exc
398	
399	    validation = validate_sketch_session(sk)
400	    if not validation.valid:
401	        raise HTTPException(
402	            status_code=409,
403	            detail={
404	                "reason": "sketch_invalid",
405	                "validation": validation.model_dump(by_alias=True),
406	            },
407	        )
408	
409	    cmds = _build_finish_commands(sk, body)
410	
411	    model_uuid = UUID(sk.model_id)
412	    row = await load_model_row(session, model_uuid)
413	    if row is None:
414	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Model not found")
415	
416	    baseline_doc = Document.model_validate(row.document)
417	    doc_before = clone_document(baseline_doc)
418	
419	    current_doc = baseline_doc
420	    last_cmd: dict[str, Any] | None = None
421	    last_violations: list[Any] = []
422	    new_doc = None
423	    for cmd in cmds:
424	        try:
425	            ok, candidate, _cmd_obj, violations, code = try_commit(current_doc, cmd)
426	        except Exception as exc:
427	            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=f"Invalid command: {exc}") from exc
428	        if not ok or candidate is None:
429	            viols_wire = [v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for v in violations]
430	            raise HTTPException(
431	                status_code=409, detail={"reason": code, "violations": viols_wire}
432	            )
433	        current_doc = candidate
434	        last_cmd = cmd
435	        last_violations = violations
436	        new_doc = candidate
437	
438	    if new_doc is None or last_cmd is None:
439	        raise HTTPException(
440	            status_code=409,
441	            detail={
442	                "reason": "sketch_invalid",
443	                "validation": {
444	                    "valid": False,
445	                    "issues": [{"code": "empty_sketch", "message": "no commands emitted"}],
446	                },
447	            },
448	        )
449	
450	    uid = body.user_id or "local-dev"
451	    undo_cmds = diff_undo_cmds(doc_before, new_doc)
452	    await delete_redos(session, model_uuid, uid)
453	    session.add(
454	        UndoStackRecord(
455	            model_id=model_uuid,
456	            user_id=uid,
457	            revision_after=new_doc.revision,
458	            forward_commands=cmds,
459	            undo_commands=undo_cmds,
460	            created_at=datetime.now(UTC),
461	        )
462	    )
463	
464	    wire_doc = document_to_wire(new_doc)
465	    row.document = wire_doc  # type: ignore[assignment]
466	    row.revision = new_doc.revision
467	    await session.commit()
468	
469	    delta = compute_delta_wire(doc_before, new_doc)
470	    if body.client_op_id:
471	        delta["clientOpId"] = body.client_op_id
472	    await hub.broadcast_json(model_uuid, {"type": "delta", "modelId": str(model_uuid), **delta})
473	
474	    sk = sk.model_copy(update={"status": "finished"})
475	    reg.replace(sk)
476	    reg.discard(session_id)
477	
478	    target_kind, id_field_name = _KIND_TO_NEW_ID_FIELD[sk.element_kind]
479	    new_ids: list[str] = [
480	        el_id
481	        for el_id, el in new_doc.elements.items()
482	        if el_id not in doc_before.elements and getattr(el, "kind", None) == target_kind
483	    ]
484	    primary_id = new_ids[0] if new_ids else None
485	
486	    response: dict[str, Any] = {
487	        "ok": True,
488	        "sessionId": session_id,
489	        "status": "finished",
490	        # Back-compat: floorId stays populated for floor sketches; new id_field
491	        # carries the kind-specific id for ceiling / roof / room_separation.
492	        "floorId": primary_id if sk.element_kind == "floor" else None,
493	        id_field_name: primary_id,
494	        "createdElementIds": new_ids,
495	        "modelId": str(model_uuid),
496	        "revision": new_doc.revision,
497	        "elements": wire_doc["elements"],
498	        "violations": violations_wire(new_doc.elements),
499	        "appliedCommand": last_cmd,
500	        "appliedCommands": cmds,
501	        "clientOpId": body.client_op_id,
502	        "delta": delta,
503	    }
504	    # Drop noise from the response when last_violations is the default empty list.
505	    _ = last_violations
506	    return response
507
